fields.description:
## Why
Audit Maintainability finding #3 (MEDIUM). The codebase reads many `REBAR_*` env vars across ~133 sites; only ~42 sit in the intended config modules â€” the rest scattered (worst in the reconciler), with no single artifact enumerating them. The repo already solved this shape (the derived prompt-index + its regenerate-and-diff CI drift gate).

## What / files
- `scripts/gen_env_registry.py`: an AST-based scan of `src/rebar/**/*.py` that resolves env-var reads through an EXPLICIT, enumerated set of read patterns (not an open-ended "find all reads" claim â€” completeness is defined RELATIVE to this recognized set, which is documented in the output and easy to extend):
  1. **Direct stdlib reads** â€” `os.environ["X"]`, `os.environ.get("X", â€¦)`, `os.getenv("X", â€¦)` with a string-literal key.
  2. **Project env-read helpers** â€” a hand-maintained `KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS` table mapping helper name â†’ (name-arg position, prefix). Resolve the string-literal name argument at each CALL SITE, one level through the shim:
     - `_rebar_env("SUFFIX", â€¦)` (reconciler, prefix `REBAR_` â†’ e.g. `REBAR_RECONCILER_VERBOSE`, `REBAR_ENV_ID`, `REBAR_AUTHOR`, `REBAR_RECONCILER_CONFLICT_PARENT_ID`, `REBAR_RECONCILER_FAIL_SILENT_NOOP`)
     - `_env_int("X", â€¦)` (outbound_differ.py, binding_store.py; no prefix)
     - `_str_pref(env_name, â€¦)` / `_int_pref(...)` (gate_source.py, janitor.py; literal first arg)
     - `_llm_int(table, cli, "REBAR_X", â€¦)` / `_llm_str` / `_llm_bool` (llm/config.py; the literal env-name argument)
  3. **Alias/deprecation status** â€” from `src/rebar/_deprecations.py` REGISTRY (`via="env"` entries: `REBAR_NO_SYNC`â†’`REBAR_SYNC_PULL`, `COMPACT_THRESHOLD`â†’`REBAR_COMPACT_THRESHOLD`). Do NOT hardcode `REBAR_PUSH`/`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` (removed pre-1.0; not live).
  The generated `docs/env-vars.md` header states exactly which read patterns/helpers were scanned (honest scope), so the registry is not misread as guaranteeing capture of a novel future indirection. A `--check` mode regenerates in-memory and exits nonzero on any diff vs the committed file. When the generator encounters a `KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS` call whose name arg is NOT a literal (unresolvable), it lists the site in a "dynamic/unresolved (see source)" section rather than dropping it silently.
- `docs/env-vars.md`: the committed generated artifact.
- `.github/workflows/test.yml`: an "Env-var registry drift gate" step modeled on the "Prompt-index drift gate" (~L224-240): run `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py --check`, failing on drift.
- A pointer to `docs/env-vars.md` in the `docs/README.md` index (Operator section).

## Scope
In: the generator (with the enumerated `KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS` resolver), the committed doc, the CI drift-gate step, the docs/README.md pointer, a unit test. Out: consolidating scattered reads into config modules; adding/removing any env var; guaranteeing capture of reads through helpers NOT in `KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS` (those are surfaced by adding the helper to the table â€” a one-line extension â€” and the doc header names the scanned set).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/env-vars.md` is committed; `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py` regenerates it with NO diff (`git diff --exit-code` clean); its header enumerates the scanned read patterns/helpers
- [ ] POSITIVE capture, asserted by a unit test (`tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py`): a direct `os.environ`/`getenv` literal read, a `_rebar_env("RECONCILER_VERBOSE")` reconciler read (â†’ `REBAR_RECONCILER_VERBOSE`), and an `_llm_int(..., "REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT", â€¦)` read ARE all present in the generated registry
- [ ] NEGATIVE/drift, asserted by the same test: a temp module adding a NEW `os.environ["REBAR_FAKE_NEW"]` read makes `--check` exit nonzero; `--check` exits 0 against the committed clean tree
- [ ] Alias/deprecation status is populated from `_deprecations.py` REGISTRY; no phantom rows for removed vars
- [ ] The drift-gate step exists in test.yml; `docs/README.md` links `docs/env-vars.md`; `make lint` green
